direction_name: Silence or underspecification around boundary cases
note: Seed example only; not part of the final direction set until derived and reviewed from CVE evidence.
description: Look for places where the standard defines normal behavior but does not clearly define malformed, extreme, out-of-range, or exceptional behavior.
reverse_inference_hint: If accepting the abnormal case would break a stated protocol goal, infer a candidate constraint requiring explicit rejection, bounded handling, or consistent normalization.

note: Seed example only; not part of the final direction set until derived and reviewed from CVE evidence.
description: Look for the same entity, field, state, or behavior described in multiple sections with inconsistent scope, strength, or boundary.
reverse_inference_hint: Infer a candidate constraint requiring consistent interpretation, or requiring the implementation to follow the stronger safety-preserving interpretation.

note: Seed example only; not part of the final direction set until derived and reviewed from CVE evidence.
description: Look for text saying an implementation may allow or support something without defining how far that support extends.
reverse_inference_hint: Infer that partial support must not silently corrupt, truncate, mis-map, or otherwise violate the protocol goal; unsupported cases should be rejected explicitly.

note: Seed example only; not part of the final direction set until derived and reviewed from CVE evidence.
description: Look for fields or mechanisms whose purpose is stated, but whose required implementation behavior is not fully specified.
reverse_inference_hint: Infer the minimum behavior needed to preserve the stated purpose, such as identity uniqueness, request-response association, anti-replay, parser termination, or cache integrity.
